title: -Zero Waste Commission Fiscal Year 2025-26 Work Plan

sections.current_situation:
The Zero Waste Commission prioritizes solid waste policy and goals, which is a
Strategic Plan Priority Project, advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-
maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities. Additionally, The City is enacting
important changes and improvements to its Zero Waste services and long-term plans,
including:
• Continuing the process to design the re-build of the Solid Waste &
Recycling Transfer Station (SW&RTS),
• Implementing various local ordinances and state laws to reduce the generation
of discarded products, packaging and materials, and to increase the recycling
of organic materials such as food scraps,
• Developing a division-level strategic plan for zero waste.
sections.background:
Zero Waste Strategic Plan: Development of the draft plan began in 2023 and is
continuing. It is anticipated that the draft plan will be ready for Council consideration of
adoption in 2024.
• SB 1383 - Food Recovery and Organics Diversion: This State of California law
requires substantial effort by local governments to reduce the landfilling of
compostable materials and to increase the recovery of otherwise discarded edible
food, as a way to reduce climate change effects associated with methane emissions
resulting from the landfilling of organic (carbon-containing) materials.

July 29, 2025
• Zero Waste Ordinances: The City has adopted various zero waste ordinances over
time. Now that their effects are being observed in practice, some of them may merit
modifications to improve their effectiveness. In addition, some new zero waste
ordinances may be helpful in guiding businesses and residents toward following zero
waste practices as standard operating procedures.
Accepted Materials in City Collection Programs: Periodically, the list of accepted
materials in City collection programs deserves review. Recycling tends to subject the
City to the vagaries of international commodity markets, with considerable swings in
demand and prices, changes in product specifications, ability to track custody and
supply chains, and end uses. Composting is not a panacea for the large variety of
hard/controversial-to-compost products and packaging, such as plastic-lined paper
and potentially compostable plastics. As with recycling, composting processors’
specifications and end uses can change over time. Meanwhile, some harder-to-
recycle materials potentially could be added to City collection services, such as
batteries.

sections.preamble:
Page 1 of 5
Office of the City Manager
INFORMATION CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
To: Honorable Mayor and Members of the City Council
From: Zero Waste Commission
Submitted by: Christienne de Tournay Birkhahn, Chair
Subject: Zero Waste Commission Fiscal Year 2025-26 Work Plan
INTRODUCTION
The Zero Waste Commission (ZWC) considers and advises City Council on solid waste
policy and goals, including refuse, composting, and recycling services, budgets, and
other decisions relating to solid waste. The ZWC adopted the FY 2024-25 Work Plan on
May 15, 2025.
